# da2 -- Python DDD & Event-Driven Architecture Framework

> da2 helps you build domain-driven Python applications with commands,
> events, entities, repositories, and a message bus -- all wired together
> via a simple bootstrap function.

## Quick Start

```python
from da2 import Entity, Command, Event, UnitOfWork, bootstrap

class CreateUser(Command):
    def __init__(self, name: str):
        self.name = name

class UserCreated(Event):
    def __init__(self, name: str):
        self.name = name

class User(Entity[str, dict]):
    pass

class MyUoW(UnitOfWork):
    def _enter(self): pass
    def _commit(self): pass
    def rollback(self): pass

def handle_create(cmd: CreateUser, uow):
    print(f"Created {cmd.name}")

bus = bootstrap(
    uow=MyUoW(),
    commands={CreateUser: handle_create},
    events={},
)
bus.handle(CreateUser(name="Alice"))
```

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Class | Purpose |
|---------|-------|---------|
| Entity | `Entity[Identity, Description]` | Domain object with identity, description, timestamps, and domain events |
| Command | `Command` | Intention to change the system (one handler) |
| Event | `Event` | Something that happened (zero or more handlers); supports `to_dict()` / `from_dict()` |
| Unit of Work | `UnitOfWork` / `UnitOfWorkAsync` | Transaction boundary; tracks seen entities and collects events |
| Message Bus | `MessageBus` / `MessageBusAsync` | Dispatches commands and events to their handlers |
| Repository | `Repository[T]` / `RepositoryAsync[T]` | Persistence abstraction with get/add/update/delete (state-based) |
| Bootstrap | `Bootstrap` / `BootstrapAsync` | Wires handlers with dependency injection |
| Container | `Container` | Simple DI container for registering and resolving dependencies |
| EventSourcedEntity | `EventSourcedEntity[Identity]` | Aggregate whose state is derived from an event stream |
| EventStore | `EventStore` / `EventStoreAsync` | Append-only event persistence with optimistic concurrency |
| RedisEventStore | `RedisEventStore` / `RedisEventStoreAsync` | Production Redis-backed event store with `load_all()` global stream (`pip install da2[redis]`) |
| EventSourcedRepository | `EventSourcedRepository[T]` / `EventSourcedRepositoryAsync[T]` | Load/save event-sourced entities via EventStore |
| Snapshot | `Snapshot` / `SnapshotStore` / `SnapshotStoreAsync` | Periodic state capture to avoid replaying all events |
| RedisSnapshotStore | `RedisSnapshotStore` / `RedisSnapshotStoreAsync` | Production Redis-backed snapshot store (`pip install da2[redis]`) |
| Projection | `Projection` / `ProjectionAsync` | CQRS read model built by applying/replaying events |
| Process Manager | `ProcessManager` / `ProcessManagerAsync` | Orchestrates multi-aggregate workflows via events -> commands |
| Policy | `Policy` / `PolicyAsync` | Stateless event-to-command reactor (no internal state) |
| Idempotency | `IdempotencyMiddleware` / `IdempotencyMiddlewareAsync` | Prevent duplicate command execution via cached results |
| RedisIdempotencyStore | `RedisIdempotencyStore` / `RedisIdempotencyStoreAsync` | Production Redis-backed idempotency store (`pip install da2[redis]`) |
| Upcaster | `Upcaster` / `UpcasterChain` | Transform stored events from older schemas to current format |

## Public API (from da2 import ...)

```
Entity, Identity, Description    # entity.py
Command                          # command.py
Event                            # event.py
UnitOfWork, UnitOfWorkAsync      # uow.py, uow_async.py
MessageBus, MessageBusAsync      # message_bus.py, message_bus_async.py
Bootstrap, BootstrapAsync        # bootstrap.py, bootstrap_async.py
bootstrap                        # __init__.py (shortcut)
Repository, RepositoryAsync      # repository.py, repository_async.py
InMemoryRepository               # repository.py
EventSourcedEntity               # event_sourced.py
EventStore, InMemoryEventStore   # event_store.py
EventStoreAsync                  # event_store_async.py
InMemoryEventStoreAsync          # event_store_async.py
RedisEventStore                  # redis_event_store.py (requires redis)
RedisEventStoreAsync             # redis_event_store_async.py (requires redis)
StoredEvent                      # event_store.py
EventSourcedRepository           # event_sourced_repository.py
EventSourcedRepositoryAsync      # event_sourced_repository_async.py
Snapshot                         # snapshot.py
SnapshotStore                    # snapshot.py
InMemorySnapshotStore            # snapshot.py
SnapshotStoreAsync               # snapshot_async.py
InMemorySnapshotStoreAsync       # snapshot_async.py
RedisSnapshotStore               # redis_snapshot_store.py (requires redis)
RedisSnapshotStoreAsync          # redis_snapshot_store_async.py (requires redis)
Projection                       # projection.py
ProjectionAsync                  # projection_async.py
ProcessManager                   # process_manager.py
ProcessManagerAsync              # process_manager_async.py
Policy                           # policy.py
PolicyAsync                      # policy_async.py
IdempotencyStore                 # idempotency.py
InMemoryIdempotencyStore         # idempotency.py
IdempotencyMiddleware            # idempotency.py
IdempotencyStoreAsync            # idempotency_async.py
InMemoryIdempotencyStoreAsync    # idempotency_async.py
IdempotencyMiddlewareAsync       # idempotency_async.py
RedisIdempotencyStore            # redis_idempotency_store.py (requires redis)
RedisIdempotencyStoreAsync       # redis_idempotency_store_async.py (requires redis)
Upcaster                         # upcaster.py
UpcasterChain                    # upcaster.py
Container                        # container.py
EntityNotFound, ConcurrencyError # exceptions.py
```

## Architecture Pattern

```
Command --> MessageBus --> CommandHandler --> UnitOfWork
                |                               |
                |                          Entity.raise_event()
                |                               |
                +--- collect_new_events() <-----+
                |
            EventHandler(s)
```

1. A Command enters the MessageBus.
2. The bus finds the single registered handler and calls it.
3. The handler uses UnitOfWork to persist changes via a Repository.
4. Entities raise domain Events during business logic.
5. UnitOfWork.collect_new_events() yields those events.
6. The bus dispatches each Event to all registered EventHandlers.
7. Events can trigger more events (chain propagation).

## Dependency Injection

Bootstrap inspects handler function signatures and auto-injects
matching dependencies by parameter name.

```python
# The "uow" parameter is injected automatically
def handle_create_user(cmd: CreateUser, uow: UnitOfWork):
    ...

bus = Bootstrap(
    uow=my_uow,
    command_handlers={CreateUser: handle_create_user},
    event_handlers={},
    dependencies={"email_service": email_svc},  # extra deps
).create_message_bus()
```

## Entity Pattern

```python
from da2 import Entity, Event

class UserCreated(Event):
    def __init__(self, user_id: str):
        self.user_id = user_id

class User(Entity[str, dict]):
    @classmethod
    def create(cls, user_id: str, name: str) -> "User":
        user = cls(identity=user_id, desc={"name": name})
        user.raise_event(UserCreated(user_id=user_id))
        return user
```

## Repository Pattern

```python
from da2 import Repository, Entity

class UserRepo(Repository[User]):
    def get(self, identity: str) -> User: ...
    def add(self, entity: User) -> None: ...
    def update(self, entity: User) -> None: ...
    def delete(self, identity: str) -> None: ...
```

For testing, use `InMemoryRepository[T]` which stores entities in a dict.

## UnitOfWork Pattern

```python
from da2 import UnitOfWork

class SqlUoW(UnitOfWork):
    def _enter(self):
        self.session = create_session()
        self.users = UserRepo(session=self.session, add_seen=self.add_seen)

    def _commit(self):
        self.session.commit()

    def rollback(self):
        self.session.rollback()
```

## Event Sourcing

da2 supports two persistence models:

1. **State-based** (Entity + Repository) -- store current state directly
2. **Event-sourced** (EventSourcedEntity + EventStore) -- store events, derive state

### EventSourcedEntity Pattern

State is built by replaying events through `_when_<EventClassName>` methods:

```python
from da2 import Event
from da2.event_sourced import EventSourcedEntity

class AccountOpened(Event):
    def __init__(self, owner: str):
        self.owner = owner

class MoneyDeposited(Event):
    def __init__(self, amount: float):
        self.amount = amount

class BankAccount(EventSourcedEntity[str]):
    def __init__(self, identity: str):
        super().__init__(identity)
        self.owner = ""
        self.balance = 0.0

    @classmethod
    def open(cls, account_id: str, owner: str) -> "BankAccount":
        account = cls(account_id)
        account._apply_and_record(AccountOpened(owner=owner))
        return account

    def deposit(self, amount: float):
        self._apply_and_record(MoneyDeposited(amount=amount))

    def _when_AccountOpened(self, event):
        self.owner = event.owner

    def _when_MoneyDeposited(self, event):
        self.balance += event.amount
```

### EventStore + EventSourcedRepository

```python
from da2.event_store import InMemoryEventStore
from da2.event_sourced_repository import EventSourcedRepository

store = InMemoryEventStore()
repo = EventSourcedRepository(
    event_store=store,
    entity_cls=BankAccount,
    event_registry={"AccountOpened": AccountOpened, "MoneyDeposited": MoneyDeposited},
)

account = BankAccount.open("acc-1", "Alice")
account.deposit(100)
repo.save(account)          # persists 2 events to store

loaded = repo.get("acc-1")  # replays events, reconstructs state
assert loaded.balance == 100.0
```

### Optimistic Concurrency

EventStore uses version-based concurrency control. If two processes
read the same aggregate and both try to save, the second save raises
`ConcurrencyError`:

```python
reader_a = repo.get("acc-1")
reader_b = repo.get("acc-1")
reader_a.deposit(50)
repo.save(reader_a)    # OK
reader_b.deposit(100)
repo.save(reader_b)    # raises ConcurrencyError
```

### Event Serialization

Events support `to_dict()` / `from_dict()` for storage:

```python
event = MoneyDeposited(amount=100)
data = event.to_dict()           # {"amount": 100}
restored = MoneyDeposited.from_dict(data)  # MoneyDeposited(amount=100)
```

### Snapshots

Snapshots avoid replaying the full event history. Configure
`snapshot_store` and `snapshot_interval` on the repository:

```python
from da2.snapshot import InMemorySnapshotStore

snap_store = InMemorySnapshotStore()
repo = EventSourcedRepository(
    event_store=store,
    entity_cls=BankAccount,
    event_registry={...},
    snapshot_store=snap_store,
    snapshot_interval=100,   # auto-snapshot every 100 events
)

# After 100+ events, repo.get() loads snapshot + recent events only
account = repo.get("acc-1")
```

Entity provides `take_snapshot()`, `from_snapshot()`, and overridable
`_snapshot_state()` / `_restore_from_snapshot()`.

## Projections (CQRS Read Models)

Projections build read-optimized views by applying domain events.
Use `_on_<EventClassName>` methods to handle specific event types:

```python
from da2 import Projection, Event, StoredEvent

class OrderPlaced(Event):
    def __init__(self, order_id: str, amount: float):
        self.order_id = order_id
        self.amount = amount

class OrderTotals(Projection):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.totals: dict[str, float] = {}

    def _on_OrderPlaced(self, event: OrderPlaced):
        self.totals[event.order_id] = event.amount
```

### Live apply vs. replay

```python
proj = OrderTotals()

# Live: apply domain events directly
proj.apply(OrderPlaced("o1", 100.0))

# Replay: rebuild from stored events
event_registry = {"OrderPlaced": OrderPlaced}
proj.replay(stored_events, event_registry)

# Catch-up: process only events after last_version
proj.catch_up(new_stored_events, event_registry)
```

`ProjectionAsync` supports async `_on_*` handlers (and mixed sync/async).

### Global Event Stream (Cross-Aggregate Projections)

For real-world CQRS, projections need events from ALL aggregates, not just one.
`EventStore.load_all()` provides a globally ordered event stream:

```python
from da2 import InMemoryEventStore, Projection

store = InMemoryEventStore()
store.append("order-1", [OrderPlaced("o1", 100.0)], expected_version=0)
store.append("user-1", [UserRegistered("u1", "Alice")], expected_version=0)

# Each event gets a global position (1, 2, 3, ...)
all_events = store.load_all()  # all events in insertion order
new_events = store.load_all(after_position=5)  # only events after position 5

# Projection can replay/catch-up from global stream
dashboard = DashboardProjection()
dashboard.replay_all(store, event_registry)       # full rebuild
dashboard.catch_up_all(store, event_registry)      # incremental
print(dashboard.last_position)                     # tracks global position
```

Key API:
- `StoredEvent.position` -- global sequence number (0 = not assigned)
- `EventStore.load_all(after_position=0)` -- read global stream
- `Projection.replay_all(event_store, registry)` -- full rebuild from global stream
- `Projection.catch_up_all(event_store, registry)` -- incremental processing
- `Projection.last_position` -- last processed global position

All store implementations support `load_all()`: `InMemoryEventStore`, `InMemoryEventStoreAsync`,
`RedisEventStore`, and `RedisEventStoreAsync`. Redis uses atomic Lua scripts with `cjson` to assign
monotonically increasing positions at write time, stored in `{prefix}:_all` (global list) and
`{prefix}:_position` (counter).

## Process Manager (Saga)

Process Managers orchestrate multi-aggregate workflows by reacting to
events and issuing commands. Use `_on_<EventClassName>` handlers and
`_dispatch(command)` to queue commands:

```python
from da2 import Command, Event
from da2.process_manager import ProcessManager

class ReserveStock(Command):
    def __init__(self, order_id: str): self.order_id = order_id

class ChargePayment(Command):
    def __init__(self, order_id: str, amount: float):
        self.order_id = order_id
        self.amount = amount

class OrderPlaced(Event):
    def __init__(self, order_id: str, amount: float):
        self.order_id = order_id
        self.amount = amount

class StockReserved(Event):
    def __init__(self, order_id: str): self.order_id = order_id

class OrderFulfillment(ProcessManager):
    def __init__(self, process_id: str):
        super().__init__(process_id)
        self.amount = 0.0

    def _on_OrderPlaced(self, event):
        self.amount = event.amount
        self._dispatch(ReserveStock(order_id=event.order_id))

    def _on_StockReserved(self, event):
        self._dispatch(ChargePayment(order_id=event.order_id, amount=self.amount))
        self._mark_completed()

pm = OrderFulfillment("order-1")
cmds = pm.handle(OrderPlaced("order-1", 99.0))
# cmds == [ReserveStock("order-1")]
```

Key API: `handle(event) -> list[Command]`, `_dispatch(cmd)`, `_mark_completed()`,
`completed` property, `process_id` property.

`ProcessManagerAsync` supports async `_on_*` handlers.

## Policy (Stateless Reactor)

Policies react to events by issuing commands, without maintaining state.
Use when you need simple "when X happens, do Y" rules:

```python
from da2 import Command, Event
from da2.policy import Policy

class SendWelcomeEmail(Command):
    def __init__(self, email: str): self.email = email

class UserRegistered(Event):
    def __init__(self, email: str): self.email = email

class WelcomePolicy(Policy):
    def _on_UserRegistered(self, event: UserRegistered):
        self._dispatch(SendWelcomeEmail(email=event.email))

policy = WelcomePolicy()
cmds = policy.handle(UserRegistered(email="alice@example.com"))
# cmds == [SendWelcomeEmail(email="alice@example.com")]
```

Policy vs ProcessManager: Policy is stateless (no process_id, no completed).
Use Policy for simple reactive rules; ProcessManager for multi-step workflows.

`PolicyAsync` supports async `_on_*` handlers.

## Middleware

Middleware wraps the entire `handle()` call on a MessageBus, enabling
cross-cutting concerns like logging, retry, validation, and error handling.

Each middleware is a callable with signature `(message, next) -> result`:

```python
def logging_mw(message, next):
    print(f">> {type(message).__name__}")
    result = next(message)
    print(f"<< {type(message).__name__}")
    return result

def retry_mw(message, next):
    for attempt in range(3):
        try:
            return next(message)
        except Exception:
            if attempt == 2:
                raise

bus = bootstrap(
    uow=my_uow,
    commands={CreateUser: handle_create},
    middleware=[logging_mw, retry_mw],
)
```

Middleware executes in order: first middleware wraps second, which wraps
the actual handler. A middleware can short-circuit (not call `next`),
modify the result, or catch exceptions.

`MessageBus.handle()` now returns the command handler's result.

For async, middleware must be async: `async def mw(message, next): ...`

Works with `Bootstrap`, `BootstrapAsync`, and the `bootstrap()` shortcut.

## Idempotent Command Handling

Prevents duplicate command execution by caching results keyed on an
`idempotency_key` attribute. Built as middleware, composable with
logging, retry, etc.

```python
from da2 import Command, bootstrap
from da2.idempotency import IdempotencyMiddleware, InMemoryIdempotencyStore

class PlaceOrder(Command):
    def __init__(self, order_id: str, idempotency_key: str = None):
        self.order_id = order_id
        self.idempotency_key = idempotency_key

store = InMemoryIdempotencyStore()
bus = bootstrap(
    uow=my_uow,
    commands={PlaceOrder: handle_place_order},
    middleware=[IdempotencyMiddleware(store, ttl_seconds=3600)],
)

# First call executes the handler
bus.handle(PlaceOrder("o1", idempotency_key="abc-123"))

# Second call with same key returns cached result (handler NOT called)
bus.handle(PlaceOrder("o1", idempotency_key="abc-123"))
```

Key behaviors:
- Commands without `idempotency_key` pass through unchanged
- Failed commands (exceptions) are NOT cached -- safe to retry
- Results cached with configurable TTL (default 1 hour)
- `InMemoryIdempotencyStore` for testing, implement `IdempotencyStore` for production

Production: `RedisIdempotencyStore` / `RedisIdempotencyStoreAsync` (requires `pip install da2[redis]`)
Async: `IdempotencyMiddlewareAsync` + `IdempotencyStoreAsync` / `InMemoryIdempotencyStoreAsync`

## Event Upcasting (Schema Evolution)

When event schemas change over time, stored events in the old format need
to be transformed to the current format during loading. Upcasters handle
this transparently -- no data migration required.

```python
from da2 import Upcaster, UpcasterChain, EventSourcedRepository

class AddCurrencyField(Upcaster):
    """v1 -> v2: OrderPlaced gained a 'currency' field."""
    event_type = "OrderPlaced"

    def upcast(self, data: dict) -> dict:
        data.setdefault("currency", "USD")
        return data

class RenameOrderPlaced(Upcaster):
    """v2 -> v3: OrderPlaced renamed to OrderCreated."""
    event_type = "OrderPlaced"
    target_event_type = "OrderCreated"

    def upcast(self, data: dict) -> dict:
        return data

chain = UpcasterChain([AddCurrencyField(), RenameOrderPlaced()])
repo = EventSourcedRepository(
    event_store=store,
    entity_cls=Order,
    event_registry={"OrderCreated": OrderCreated},
    upcaster_chain=chain,
)
order = repo.get("order-1")  # legacy events are upcasted on the fly
```

Key design:
- `Upcaster`: abstract base class with `event_type` and `upcast(data) -> data`
- `UpcasterChain`: applies upcasters in registration order
- Optional `target_event_type` renames the event type after upcasting
- Input data is copied (originals not mutated)
- Works with both `EventSourcedRepository` and `EventSourcedRepositoryAsync`

## Async Support

All sync classes have async counterparts:
- `UnitOfWorkAsync` (async context manager)
- `MessageBusAsync` (await bus.handle(...))
- `RepositoryAsync[T]` (async get/add/update/delete)
- `BootstrapAsync` (produces MessageBusAsync)
- `EventStoreAsync` / `InMemoryEventStoreAsync`
- `EventSourcedRepositoryAsync[T]`
- `SnapshotStoreAsync` / `InMemorySnapshotStoreAsync`
- `ProjectionAsync` (async/sync mixed handlers)
- `ProcessManagerAsync` (async/sync mixed handlers)
- `PolicyAsync` (async/sync mixed handlers)

## Install

```
pip install da2
```

## Links

- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/da2/
- GitHub: https://github.com/warren-wupeng/da2
- License: MIT
